name: Fenty Beauty
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Fenty Beauty's brand language — Rihanna's inclusive beauty empire that redrew the cosmetics industry around radical shade range. Canvas is pure black (#0a0a0a) or near-black with deep saturated ink. Type runs a precise grotesk display (Söhne / Inter / proprietary all-caps face) often paired with an italic serif accent. The voice is luxurious, club-night, unapologetically Black and unapologetically Caribbean — gold filigree on black, full-bleed product hero shots, model casting that spans every Pantone of human skin. The signature 50-shade Pro Filt'r foundation grid IS the brand — it appears on hero, product, and editorial pages as a strip of swatches that demonstrates the brand's core promise visually before any copy lands.

design_language.motion_signature: Hero video plays product close-ups — gloss bottles tilting under spotlight, foundation pumps swirling. Shade swatches reveal sequentially on scroll (~80ms cascade between swatches). Hover on product tiles raises a subtle gold-stroke border over 200ms. The brand voice is 'spotlight reveal' — every motion implies a magazine cover shoot.

trademark_signals: 50-shade foundation grid — a horizontal strip of swatches from the lightest to the deepest in the Pro Filt'r range — used as the brand-promise visual on every page, before any copy
Pure-black canvas with gold filigree accent — the gold (#d4af37) appears only on the wordmark, on category dividers, and on inline 'NEW' tags — luxury without saturation overload
Editorial model casting that spans the entire skin-tone spectrum — every model imagery moment shows multiple skin tones together, not as 'diversity feature' but as default
All-caps display typography with wide letter-spacing on hero headlines — 'FENTY ICON' set at 80px with +4% tracking reads as fashion-week-poster, not e-commerce-hero
Product photography on jet-black with gold-accent rim-light — every gloss bottle, every powder palette shot with the same studio setup so the product catalog reads as a unified hero gallery
ai_slop_avoided: Don't drop the 50-shade strip. The brand-promise visualization is the single most copyable Fenty asset — a generic 'inclusive beauty' line without the swatch strip reads as imitation, not Fenty
Don't paint the canvas in cream/beige. The pure-black canvas IS the luxury voltage; pale-pink or cream canvas (Glossier's territory) reads as a different brand entirely
Don't saturate the gold. The gold (#d4af37) is muted-warm, never bright-yellow; bright yellow reads as commercial, not editorial
Don't soften the all-caps typography. The display tier is locked at +2 to +4% tracking — too-tight reads as off-brand
Don't homogenize model casting. Single-model imagery loses the brand's defining message; every model moment should show range
